{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)


1. Introduction and Purpose (GRI 102)

This report documents the technical and ethical transition of the **Bokitas Ver 2.6** health control system to a modern data architecture. The process is governed by the **GRI 203 (Indirect Economic Impacts)** standard by strengthening community health infrastructure and the **GRI 418 (Customer Privacy)** standard by ensuring the protection of sensitive beneficiary data.

2. Forensic Data Archaeology (Phase 1)

Forensic engineering was performed on a legacy Visual FoxPro database, rescuing the relational integrity of historical operations.

##

Inventory of Rescued Tables
| Module | Legacy Table | Records | Critical Function |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Nutritional** | `tb_antropometrica` | 528 | Longitudinal measurement tracking |
| **Socioeconomic** | `tb_socioeconomico` | 221 | Graffar Stratification |
| **Clinical** | `tb_pediatrica` | 412 | Medical diagnosis and treatment |
| **Reference** | `tb_pesotalla...` | 1,200 | WHO Standards (SD-3 to SD+3) |

3. Linear Processing Methodology (Tidyverse)

Historical data normalization was designed using functional programming principles to ensure reproducibility.

{r cleaning-logic}

Simulation of cleaning and consolidation of Master Data
master_data <- tb_antropometrica %>%
inner_join(tb_menores, by = "cedula") %>%
mutate(
fecha_cons = as.Date(fecha_ini),
peso_kg = as.numeric(peso),
talla_cm = as.numeric(talla)
) %>%
filter(!is.na(peso_kg) & talla_cm > 0) %>%
select(cedula, fecha_cons, peso_kg, talla_cm, sexo, edad_meses)


4. Impact Indicator Calculation (GRI 203)

#

4.1. Anthropometric Analysis (WHO Anthro)
Following **WHO (2006)** guidelines, the system integrates Z-Score calculations to determine the standard deviation of the population.

{r z-score-calc}

Logic integration with WHO standard
library(anthro)

indices_nutri <- anthro_zscores(
sex = master_data$sexo,
age = master_data$edad_meses,
weight = master_data$peso_kg,
lenhei = master_data$talla_cm
)

Binary classification for management reports
master_data <- master_data %>%
mutate(dnt_aguda = ifelse(indices_nutri$zwei < -2, "Alert", "Normal"))


#

4.2. Socioeconomic Stratification (Graffar)
The data engine applies the **Graffar-Méndez Castellano** method to cross-reference social vulnerability with nutritional status.

{r graffar-logic}

Assignment of scores by housing and education variables
social_summary <- tb_socioeconomico %>%
group_by(estrato) %>%
summarise(
total_beneficiaries = n(),
risk_rate = mean(puntaje > 15)
)


5. Privacy Shielding and GRI 418

In compliance with the **GRI 418-1** standard, a strict anonymization protocol was implemented before any cloud environment synchronization.

##

Mitigation Actions Performed
- **Anonymization:** Replacement of real ID numbers and names with unique alphanumeric identifiers (UUIDs).
- **Isolation:** Physical removal of original `.DBF` files from the Git-tracked repository.
- **Dynamic Security:** Implementation of `.env` environment variables for database credentials.

6. Technical Assurance (Certification)

> ###

TECHNICAL DISCLAIMER
> **It is certified that this repository is free of Personally Identifiable Information (PII).** All data contained in the development folders (`mock_data/`) is synthetic and does not correspond to real individuals. The Git history has been audited to ensure no residual metadata from the legacy archaeology phase exists.

---

**Signature:**
*Lead Data Scientist & Compliance Officer*
*Bokitas Data Intelligence Project*
